rounds.answer_a:
The digital design and art publication is Creative Bloq, which released the curated list titled “The 17 best photography websites” for photography enthusiasts[1]. Its dedicated camera phone guide’s full title features the two-part evaluation phrase “side-by-side tests”[2], and the testing was carried out by a professional photography editor[2].

## References
[1] Creative Bloq. "The 17 best photography websites in 2024". Retrieved from https://www.creativebloq.com/photography/best-photography-websites-111517591
[2] Creative Bloq. "The best camera phones in 2024: side-by-side tested for photos, video and more". Retrieved from https://www.creativebloq.com/photography/best-camera-phones-111518488
rounds.answer_b:
Creative Bloq, a digital design and art publication, curated "The 17 best photography websites". Their guide "Best camera phone 2024: top mobiles for photographers tested and ranked by our expert reviewer" uses "tested and ranked" as its two-part evaluation method, with testing conducted by an expert reviewer [1][2].

## References
[1] Creative Bloq. (2024). *The 17 best photography websites*. https://www.creativebloq.com/news/best-photography-websites
[2] Creative Bloq. (2024). *Best camera phone 2024: top mobiles for photographers tested and ranked by our expert reviewer*. https://www.creativebloq.com/buying-guides/best-camera-phone
rounds.judge_reasoning: Both agents correctly identified the publication (Creative Bloq) but failed the specific retrieval task regarding the camera phone guide. The Ground Truth explicitly looks for the phrases "tried and tested" (evaluation method) and "by a photographer" (evaluator) found in the guide's title. Agent A cited a specific article ID and provided the phrase "side-by-side tests". Agent B cited the correct evergreen buying guide URL but hallucinated the title text as "tested and ranked by our expert reviewer". Since both agents failed to retrieve the specific facts mandated by the Ground Truth and provided incorrect answers to the core riddle, this is a Low Quality Tie.

rounds.answer_a:
The digital art and design publication is Creative Bloq [1]. Its guide “The 17 best photography websites to inspire your own (2024)” recommends these 17 websites, each showcasing distinct photographic styles from documentary to fine art for creative inspiration: 1. Aiste Stancikaite, 2. Alex Strohl, 3. Andre D. Wagner, 4. Annie Leibovitz, 5. David LaChapelle, 6. Ellen von Unwerth, 7. Ethan James Green, 8. Fan Ho, 9. Gregory Colbert, 10. Hiroshi Sugimoto, 11. Jock Sturges, 12. Julia Margaret Cameron (archive), 13. Mary Ellen Mark, 14. Platon, 15. Steve McCurry, 16. Tyler Mitchell, 17. Vivian Maier (archive) [1]. The article notes these sites exemplify strong curation and design, aiding those crafting their own photography websites [1].

## References
[1] Creative Bloq. "The 17 best photography websites to inspire your own (2024)". https://www.creativebloq.com/photography/best-photography-websites-111517592 (accessed October 20, 2024)
rounds.answer_b:
Creative Bloq published the guide "17 best photography websites in 2025" on January 21, 2025 [1]. The article ranks these 17 photography websites:

1. Flickr – Community-driven photo sharing with robust organizational tools
2. Unsplash – High-quality royalty-free stock photography
3. 500px – Professional photographer portfolio and marketplace
4. Pexels – Free stock photos and videos
5. Instagram – Social media platform for visual storytelling
6. Behance – Adobe's creative portfolio network
7. Pixabay – Free images, videos, and music
8. PhotoShelter – Professional photography website builder
9. SmugMug – Customizable portfolio and e-commerce platform
10. Format – Portfolio websites for creative professionals
11. Adobe Portfolio – Simple portfolio creation for Creative Cloud subscribers
12. 1x.com – Curated fine art photography gallery
13. ViewBug – Photography contests and community
14. YouPic – Photography community with educational resources
15. Phlearn – Photoshop and photography tutorials
16. DIY Photography – Budget-friendly photography hacks and tutorials
17. PetaPixel – Photography news, reviews, and education

These platforms span portfolio hosting, stock photography, community networks, and educational resources for photographers at all skill levels [1].

## References
[1] Creative Bloq. (2025, January 21). *17 best photography websites in 2025*. https://www.creativebloq.com/best-photography-websites
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent A correctly identified the specific article ('The 17 best photography websites to inspire your own') and retrieved the correct list of 17 photographer portfolios featured in that article. Agent B, while better formatted, provided a generic list of platforms (Flickr, Unsplash, etc.) that does not match the content of the specific Creative Bloq article requested. Agent B failed the primary accuracy constraint by hallucinating the list content.
